markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games has prioritized quality over speed for Grand Theft Auto VI, driving the latest shift to a November 19, 2026 release after missing an earlier May target. This follows two prior delays from the original 2025 window, with the studio citing the need for additional polish on its massive open-world title. Take-Two Interactive’s May 2026 earnings call stands as the next major checkpoint, where traders will watch for any fresh signals on development progress or further tweaks. Momentum remains steady around the current date, supported by Rockstar’s track record of delivering high-profile titles without last-minute slips once dates are locked. A third trailer or pre-order details could still emerge to clarify the campaign timeline ahead of launch.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders assign overwhelming implied probability to no additional prison time for Harvey Weinstein because he already faces lengthy concurrent sentences from his California rape conviction and prior New York matters, with the current retrial charge carrying a maximum of just four years. Jury deliberations in the Manhattan retrial began this week after opening statements and testimony revisited the 2013 Jessica Mann allegations, yet the 74-year-old producer’s declining health—marked by recent chest pains in court and extended time at Rikers—has shifted focus toward possible plea resolutions or time-served considerations. Historical patterns in high-profile sexual assault retrials and the limited upside of new sentencing relative to existing terms reinforce the market’s consensus that any outcome will not meaningfully extend his incarceration.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Colorado Avalanche and Carolina Hurricanes remain neck-and-neck as the top contenders for the 2026 Stanley Cup because both clubs have delivered elite regular-season records and continued that momentum deep into the playoffs. The Avalanche captured the Presidents’ Trophy with the league’s best overall mark, while the Hurricanes completed a first-round sweep and stayed unbeaten heading into the Eastern Conference semifinals. This head-to-head strength has kept their implied probabilities nearly identical in trader consensus. The Vegas Golden Knights sit further back after advancing from a competitive Western series, and the Montreal Canadiens and Buffalo Sabres trail after their own second-round matchup, limiting their paths despite solid early postseason results. The bunched pricing reflects how quickly any of these four teams could shift the field with a strong conference-final run.

The Avalanche captured the Presidents’ Trophy with the league’s best overall mark, while the Hurricanes completed a first-round sweep and stayed unbeaten heading into the Eastern Conference semifinals. This head-to-head strength has kept their implied probabilities nearly identical in trader consensus. The Vegas Golden Knights sit further back after advancing from a competitive Western series, and the Montreal Canadiens and Buffalo Sabres trail after their own second-round matchup, limiting their paths despite solid early postseason results. The bunched pricing reflects how quickly any of these four teams could shift the field with a strong conference-final run.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Oklahoma City Thunder hold a commanding lead in 2026 NBA champion odds due to their dominant regular-season record, league-best depth, and undefeated 8-0 playoff run capped by a sweep of the Lakers that advanced them to the Western Conference Finals behind Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's elite scoring and playmaking. Cleveland, Detroit, and Minnesota remain distant long shots given tougher remaining paths and less consistent recent results against top competition.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France and Spain remain narrowly ahead of England in the 2026 FIFA World Cup title market due to their deep squads and recent European Championship pedigree, with France holding a marginal edge after Spain's 18-year-old star Lamine Yamal suffered a hamstring injury that briefly shifted implied probabilities. Norway's inclusion of Erling Haaland has elevated its standing among mid-tier contenders, underscoring how roster health and qualifying momentum continue to compress odds among the leading nations less than a month before the expanded 48-team tournament begins.

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France and Spain remain narrowly ahead of England in the 2026 FIFA World Cup title market due to their deep squads and recent European Championship pedigree, with France holding a marginal edge after Spain's 18-year-old star Lamine Yamal suffered a hamstring injury that briefly shifted implied probabilities. Spain's unbeaten run since March 2024 and Argentina's intact core from the 2022 triumph keep the top five tightly grouped, while Brazil and Portugal benefit from star power and favorable group draws. Norway's inclusion of Erling Haaland has elevated its standing among mid-tier contenders, underscoring how roster health and qualifying momentum continue to compress odds among the leading nations less than a month before the expanded 48-team tournament begins.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping's entrenched dominance as Communist Party general secretary, reinforced by loyalist appointments across the Politburo and military leadership, underpins trader expectations that he will remain in office through the 21st Party Congress in late 2027. Recent early-2026 purges of senior PLA generals and defense officials via anti-corruption campaigns have further centralized authority rather than exposing vulnerability, while the absence of any designated successor or visible factional opposition sustains the pattern of extended rule established after the 2018 removal of presidential term limits. Ongoing public appearances and diplomatic scheduling, including anticipated high-level engagements into 2026, align with this continuity. The 93 percent "No" pricing reflects broad consensus that institutional and personnel controls make early removal improbable absent a major unforeseen shock.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following the 2024 presidential contest, the Democratic nomination for 2028 remains highly fluid, with trader consensus placing Gavin Newsom at the front due to his high-profile role as California governor and consistent national media presence on issues like climate policy and healthcare. Kamala Harris holds secondary positioning from her recent vice presidential tenure and prior nomination, though her support has moderated amid party reflection on recent results. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez draws backing from progressive circles emphasizing economic equity and social justice priorities, while senators and governors like Jon Ossoff and Josh Shapiro appeal through legislative records and battleground state credentials. Key differentiators include executive experience versus legislative or activist backgrounds, with consolidation likely hinging on early fundraising hauls, state-level polling trends, and formal campaign announcements ahead of primary contests.

Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez draws backing from progressive circles emphasizing economic equity and social justice priorities, while senators and governors like Jon Ossoff and Josh Shapiro appeal through legislative records and battleground state credentials. Key differentiators include executive experience versus legislative or activist backgrounds, with consolidation likely hinging on early fundraising hauls, state-level polling trends, and formal campaign announcements ahead of primary contests.

markets.question: Will Gina Raimondo win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.
